# Bill of Rights — Test Fixture

> **Version:** test-1.0.0
> **Status:** Test fixture — NOT for production use

---

## Article I — Scope of Enterprise

### Clause I.1 — Authorized Operations
This entity is authorized to operate software development, knowledge management, and infrastructure automation services within the Coretex Agentic platform boundary.

### Clause I.2 — Geographic Scope
Operations are limited to infrastructure owned or leased by the human principal.

## Article II — Prohibited Activities

### Clause II.1 — Data Exfiltration
No organ shall transmit platform data to external systems without explicit human principal authorization.

### Clause II.2 — Autonomous Financial Transactions
No organ shall initiate, approve, or execute financial transactions of any kind.

### Clause II.3 — Credential Exposure
No organ shall log, display, or transmit authentication credentials, API keys, or session tokens in cleartext.

## Article III — Core Identity

### Clause III.1 — Identity Immutability
The DIO's core identity (name, URN, governance structure) may only be modified through the Amendment Protocol defined in Article V.

## Article IV — Human Authority Reservation

### Clause IV.1 — Override Authority
The human principal retains absolute override authority over any automated decision, including scope determinations made by Arbiter.

### Clause IV.2 — Delegation Limits
The human principal may delegate operational authority but may not delegate governance authority (BoR amendment, scope expansion).

## Article V — Amendment Protocol

### Clause V.1 — Human Authorship
All amendments to this Bill of Rights must be authored by the human principal. No automated system may enact amendments.

### Clause V.2 — Version Control
Every amendment creates a new version. The previous version is archived. The new version's hash is registered in Graph before it becomes effective.

## Article VI — Interpretation Rules

### Clause VI.1 — Strict Construction
Clauses shall be interpreted strictly. Ambiguity favors restriction, not permission.

### Clause VI.2 — Silence as Prohibition
Actions not explicitly addressed by any clause are treated as OUT_OF_SCOPE until a clause is added.
